subject: [SO] Sync up with the last-minute MSB certificate changes.
commit_message:
[SO] Sync up with the last-minute MSB certificate changes.

JKS file for SO Sol003 adapter updated to reflect changes in MSB. White spaces removed in override file which caused issues.

Issue-ID: SO-2982
Change-Id: I78181cbba6fb1d785eb503bd1e9de5f9bb8a9b4a
Signed-off-by: Mukesh <mukeshsharma@est.tech>

Patch Set 1:

Base on G release requirements there should be no hardcoded certificates in both docker image and OOM repo and everyone should use certInitializer instead. Thus before we consider merging this I'd like to make sure that you acknowledge that and would be great to have a jira ticket tracing this

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Base on G release requirements there should be no hardcoded certificates in both docker image and OOM repo and everyone should use certInitializer instead. Thus before we consider merging this I'd like to make sure that you acknowledge that and would be great to have a jira ticket tracing this

Hi Krzysztof, Thanks! This check-in is to fix the SOL003 Adapter secure communication with the ETSI Catalog Manager through MSB in the Frankfurt. Due to the last-minute MSB certificate change, the tested secured communication has been broken. This fix will sync up the MSB certificate for Frankfurt to make the communication between the SOL003 Adapter and ETSI Catalog Manager works again. We put the same fixes to both Frankfurt (part of the Frankfurt maintenance release) and Guilin (for now). We acknowledge that Guilin provides additional security requirements and we will conform to the changes. For the Guilin, this is the base and we will conform the Guilin security mechanism.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Hello,
Ramesh is right:
the commit message (on OOM) must follow this form:

[NAME_OF_COMPONENT|DOC|COMMON|GENERIC] Meaningful title (from OOM side)

at least one sentence explaining the change done in this patch, cause and consequences and possibly more of course

Issue-ID: AS_WE_ARE_FORCED_BUT_MEANINGLESS
Change-ID: xxx
Sign-off: xxx

Commit message will be the last stuff that will stay with our code so it must clearly explain the changes, the "why" and the consequences. If it change OOM behavior in any way, documentation must be also updated.

Starting Frankfurt branching, merge requests which are not following this pattern will not be merged.

Please read the following pages and follow the guidelines for writing commit message contained therein.

    http://bit.ly/goodcommitmessages
    http://who-t.blogspot.com/2009/12/on-commit-messages.html
    http://dep.debian.net/deps/dep3/

Patch Set 1:

For MSB part, as long as MSB is not covered by AAF (hoping it will come soon), I understand that you need the hardcoded truststore so it's OK.

Patch Set 1:

didn't saw krzysztof comment sorry.
I'd like to have a JIRA ticket number for MSB (as they have to go under "AAF" umbrella) and SO (as we need to retrieve onap truststore automatically here).

We can help on second (and first one) to solve. 

it should be rather simple for second one (as it seems you already have a config in AAF), but first one needs to be completed

Hope this satisfies you guys. 
Thanks.

Byung
